contracts.acceleratedPath: WGSL RK4 ensemble kernel in src/runtime/gpuEnsemble.ts
contracts.acceptanceRule: GPU integration may feed research outputs only through validated statistics; CPU force path remains the trajectory oracle.
contracts.caveat: Node CI has no real adapter; hardware WebGPU runs must still report backend=webgpu and caveat=f32.

contracts.acceleratedPath: webgpuEnsembleStatistics GPU reduction or f32 candidate reduction
contracts.acceptanceRule: compareEnsembleStatistics(candidate, cpuOracle) must pass declared tolerances before a reduction can be used as a publication result.
contracts.caveat: The local CI candidate is f32-rounded CPU output; the self-hosted hardware workflow runs the on-device reduction when a WebGPU adapter is present.

contracts.acceleratedPath: WGSL flip-basin / sweep / FTLE field kernels
contracts.acceptanceRule: GPU output is accepted only when deterministic CPU probe validation passes; otherwise the f64 CPU grid is returned.
contracts.caveat: Fractal basin boundaries allow isolated probe disagreements; the gate is on disagreement fraction.

contracts.acceleratedPath: GPU or parallel candidate that emits the same public result schema
contracts.acceptanceRule: GPU candidates must pass compareClvAcceleration / compareFtleFieldAcceleration / compareLyapunovSpectrumAcceleration against the CPU oracle before promotion.
contracts.caveat: The acceleration contract is implemented; the default production CLV/full-spectrum kernels remain CPU until a hardware candidate passes it.

f32ReductionOracle.caveat: This validates ensemble reduction statistics only; trajectory integration and chaotic divergence still require their own CPU probe gates.

lyapunovSpectrum.caveat: Compares finite-time spectrum outputs for identical settings; does not certify a different integration horizon or seed.

clv.caveat: Compares sign-invariant CLV summary quantities; individual vector signs and ordering must be handled by the caller.

ftleField.caveat: Compares like-for-like FTLE fields. Variational STM and finite-difference flow-map fields must not be mixed without a method caveat.
